replies_posted.reply_text: Yeah but can he win a chip with the Warriors? 😐
Steph's got 4 rings, though. That's a big gap between him and MJ
Without Steph, I think we see some more Klay. He's been working on his shot, let's see it tonight
Yeah 5 blocks is big time. That Pelicans' frontcourt is a mess 🤣
Yeah, been a little inconsistent in March, but still putting up MVP numbers
Yeah those are crazy numbers. He's a great fit for their offense
Nah, he's been doing that all year. You're just now noticing
Not great for the Magic. Richardson's been playing well lately
I agree. If you're one of the best, you should be available most of the time. It's a strength not a weakness
D2? That's not much of a stretch for a kid with his skillset and size. He could easily go higher
Yeah they need to try something new. Pacers offense has been stagnant too, not just defense
Exactly, that one to Miller was a setup. Officials gotta be more consistent
14.5 points is a huge spread. Still think Warriors can pull it off without Steph
Love Shaq but he's gotta go for Hakeem. That's my all-time C
Still getting robbed of MVPs
Imagine the stress on his legs. Unbelievable shooter.
Finally some honest officiating. Hawks were getting too many softies
KD's size for a 4 is insane but Shaq's dominance makes the C spot a lock
Tears of joy? 😊 Wemby's gonna be a star, no need to cry about it
Wolves should've closed it out, those 3s killed them. Oliveira is a stud, can't believe they benched him for so long
Darius Acuff? That's not a real name. Not buying this hypothetical 😒
Nikola Jokic tied for 7th? Not shocking, still doing it all for Denver.
Jefferson went to Georgetown, not a Cavaliers school. Georgetown vs Maryland rivalry tho
That's a sick find! Wilkins would've fit in great in today's NBA
That's cute but 03 Nuggets didn't have Melo's talent. Still a long shot for them this year
Fair point, it's not a direct comparison. MJ's era was way more dominant overall tho
Yeah, but was there a better offer? Maybe they saw something we don't
That's insane! He's been dominating the Hawks in the playoffs for years
Exactly. LaMelo's impact goes way beyond the offense, though. Their defense is whole different animal when he's on the court
Yeah, that's why they're gonna get blown by Boston. No way he's ready for Tatum
Thought process? He just threw it down 😂
Story matters, data points help but ultimately it's about fit and need. Not just some generic archetypes
Curry's the best shooter of all time. Don't @ me
Good to see Kawhi back after a tough 37 minutes last night. Should be interesting to see how he holds up in Memphis tonight
That's a big deal for Memphis. They need him back ASAP
Good pick on Kawhi, but Jalen Duren's all-around game is underrated
Whole team injured? Against the Nets? That's just cruel
True, Wagler's game control is underrated. Plus, he's been getting those tough fouls, too
Still don't think that's a knock on KD. LeBron got crazy hot in those series, but KD's impact was still huge
Yeah, he's been setting up his teammates all season. That vision is elite
Wemby taking over in San Antonio, love the way he's dominating
Love the honesty from Jaylen Brown. Wemby's ceiling is unreal
Lol yeah he's on a mission. 22 points in 3Q already
Not buying Spurs being ready now. They still have a lot to figure out defensively
Agree, dominant force on the court. Especially when Embiid is out
Nah MJ had that extra gear in the 88 Olympics. Different level of dominance
Chuck's all-around game was elite back then, but Nash's court vision was unparalleled
Haven't been impressed by their bench scoring so far. They'll need more from Murray's backups
They did get Luka to 28% from 3 though. Still a huge win for Boston
Maxey's a rookie, cut him some slack. That turnover was a rookie mistake
Man that sequence still gives me chills, his will to win is insane
